Scenario.title: Energy Sector Phishing Attack Disrupting Grid Operations
Scenario.overview: Energy Simple A low-skill threat group leverages phishing and known vulnerabilities in energy sector applications to manipulate critical operational data and destabilize grid systems.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Application Log, OS Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Application Log Content (Mechanism), Data Manipulation

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker deploys phishing emails containing malicious links or attachments to trick energy sector employees into revealing credentials. These credentials are then used to access internet-facing systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Using stolen credentials, the attacker exploits vulnerabilities in unpatched energy sector applications by manipulating application logs to execute arbitrary code and gain unauthorized access.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker alters operational data within critical OS processes to compromise grid stability, causing unplanned outages or systemic disruptions.

Scenario.summary: A basic-skilled threat group compromises an energy sector organization through phishing, exploits vulnerable applications, and manipulates operational data to disrupt grid infrastructure.
